name: wishbone_reg_block
description: Small Wishbone-compatible register block with address decode, read mux, write strobes, acknowledge, and optional interrupt/status bits.

source_refs.license: BSD-like OpenCores license in source header
source_refs.notes: Concrete Wishbone register decode for I2C control/status registers.

source_refs.license: OpenCores project license; verify file header before reuse
source_refs.notes: Concrete Wishbone register decode for SPI control/status registers.

states.name: ACK
states.description: Acknowledge the selected register access for one cycle.

constraints: Acknowledge each accepted Wishbone transaction exactly according to the selected wait-state policy.
Writes must not overwrite read-only hardware status fields.
One-shot command bits need explicit clear behavior after hardware consumption.
Read mux must return deterministic values for reserved addresses.
patterns: Define write access as cyc & stb & we & ack or cyc & stb & we depending on wait-state policy.
Generate ack as a registered one-cycle pulse to avoid combinational bus loops.
Use a case statement for read mux and write decode.
Clear write-one-to-clear command/interrupt bits explicitly after hardware consumes them.
implementation_notes: Derive a write-access strobe from cyc, stb, we, and the acknowledge policy.
Use a registered ack pulse to avoid combinational loops into the bus fabric.
Keep write decode and read mux in separate always/case blocks for reviewability.
Document reset values for every software-visible register.
test_strategy: Write every register and read back writable fields.
Check read-only status fields are not overwritten by writes.
Verify ack timing for read and write cycles.
verification_goals: Every documented writable register can be written and read back where applicable.
Read-only status fields reflect hardware inputs and ignore software writes.
ack timing is one pulse per bus access.
Command and interrupt clear bits have no unintended sticky behavior.
